poly.description: Polygon defining the area of interest. Counter-clockwise array of {lon, lat} objects forming a closed ring (first point must equal last point).

region_mask.description: Rasterized region for complex polygons with holes or non-convex boundaries. More performant than polygon for point-in-region tests on complex shapes. The poly parameter is still required for granule discovery — region_mask only refines which points within discovered granules are returned.

geojson.description: GeoJSON string defining the region of interest.

proj.description: Projection. Auto selects based on latitude (polar stereographic for high latitudes, appropriate projection for mid-latitudes).

datum.description: Vertical datum. When unspecified, heights are WGS84 ellipsoidal.

max_resources.description: Maximum number of HDF5 granules the server will process for a single request. Narrow the polygon or time window to stay under this limit, or increase it for comprehensive coverage.

resources.description: Explicit list of granule filenames to process. Bypasses CMR spatial/temporal discovery.

points_in_polygon.description: Limit the number of points returned within the polygon. 0 means no limit. Useful for quick data previews.

cluster_size_hint.description: Hint to the server about expected workload size for cluster scaling. 0 means automatic.

key_space.description: Internal key space for parallel processing. -1 means auto-assigned. Only override for client-side request parallelization.

output.description: Output format configuration. For direct HTTP API usage, always include format, as_geo, and with_checksum.

path.description: Output file path (used by Python client).

asset.description: Output asset target for server-side storage.

credentials.description: AWS credentials for server-side output to S3. Not needed for direct HTTP API usage.

output.note: X-series APIs return time_ns (Unix nanoseconds) as the DataFrame index. Always call reset_index() after to_pandas() to preserve the time column.

samples.description: Dictionary of raster datasets to sample at each output point. Each key becomes the column name prefix in the output (e.g., 'mosaic' produces 'mosaic.value', 'mosaic.time_ns', 'mosaic.file_id'). Cross-domain: applies to every X-series API.

asset.description: Asset name from SlideRule's asset directory. The authoritative list is served by /source/assets on a running SlideRule server.

radius.description: Sampling kernel radius. Also defines the region for zonal statistics.

zonal_stats.description: Compute and return zonal statistics (count, min, max, mean, median, stdev, MAD) for the sampling region.

with_flags.description: Include 32-bit auxiliary pixel flags from the raster.

bands.description: Specific bands to read. Some assets support derived bands.

t0.description: Start time filter for temporal raster datasets.

t1.description: Stop time filter for temporal raster datasets.

use_poi_time.description: Use the point-of-interest timestamp for closest-time raster matching instead of explicit t0/t1.

catalog.description: GeoJSON STAC catalog for datasets requiring external catalog lookup (e.g., usgs3dep-1meter-dem).

arcticdem-strips.description: ArcticDEM individual time-stamped strips (PGC, polar north).

rema-mosaic.description: Reference Elevation Model of Antarctica composite mosaic (PGC, polar south).

rema-strips.description: Reference Elevation Model of Antarctica individual time-stamped strips (PGC, polar south).

esa-worldcover-10meter.description: ESA WorldCover 10m global land classification (2021 v200).

esa-copernicus-30meter.description: Copernicus DEM 30m global. Replaces the legacy cop-dem alias.

3dep1m.description: Alternate registration of USGS 3DEP 1-meter source data.

bluetopo-bathy.description: NOAA BlueTopo coastal bathymetric tile collection (CONUS).

gedil3-canopy.description: GEDI L3 canopy height raster (RH100 mean, 2019108-2022019).

gedil3-canopy-stddev.description: GEDI L3 canopy height standard deviation raster.

gedil3-elevation-stddev.description: GEDI L3 elevation standard deviation raster.

gedil4b.description: GEDI L4B gridded aboveground biomass density raster.

user-url-raster.description: Generic raster sampled from a user-provided URL. Use for ad-hoc rasters not in the SlideRule asset registry.

output_columns.note: For multi-valued rasters (multiple strips or bands), column values are arrays. A file_directory in the DataFrame metadata maps file_id to raster filenames.

samples.note: Authoritative list of assets at runtime is /source/assets on the SlideRule server. The known_assets registry above is documentation only.
